DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE PARTIES DID NOT STIPULATE TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION PREPARED. DEFENDANT AHN AND TODD OPPENHEIMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF THE SUBPOENA FOR IHSS'S RECORDS IS CONTINUED TO MAY 2, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
ORDER STRIKING CERTAIN EXPERTS ON DEFENDANTS' SUPPLEMENTAL EXPERT DESIGNATION AND DENYING MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO OTHERS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AHN AND TODD OPPENHEIMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF THE SUBPOENA FOR IHSS' RECORDS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOW GOOD CAUSE AS TO WHY DISCOVERY SHOULD BE PERMITTED PAST THE DISCOVERY CUT OFF DATE. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT VIKTOR SHOYKHET RECEIVED THE REQUIRED NOTIFICATION THAT HIS EMPLOYMENT RECORDS WERE SOUGHT. THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA IS OVERLY BROAD. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE AWARDED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS TODD OPPENHEIMER AND ANH OPPENHEIMER'S MOTION FOR MEDICAL EXAMINATIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY TO TAKE THE MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF. (CCP 2024.050(B).) DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN DILIGENCE IN PROCEEDING WITH DISCOVERY. DEFENDANTS WERE AWARE THAT PLAINTIFF'S MENTAL CONDITION WAS AT ISSUE MONTHS PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THIS ACTION AND THAT THE MEDICAL RECORDS REVIEWED BY DR. RUFF WERE MADE AVAILABLE MANY MONTHS BEFORE HE WAS FIRST ENGAGED AS AN EXPERT BY THE DEFENSE. DEFENDANTS' DELAY IN COMMENCING REVIEW OF THE MEDICAL RECORDS MADE AVAILABLE BY THE PLAINTIFFS IS INCONSISTENT WITH THE LEVEL OF DILIGENCE WHICH WOULD WARRANT RE-OPENING DISCOVERY. (SEE, E.G., JOHNSON V. ALAMEDA COUNTY MEDICAL CENTER (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 521 (WHEN THE MOVING PARTY DELAYS COMMENCING DISCOVERY, THE TRIAL COURT DID NOT ABUSE DISCRETION BY REFUSING TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY).) FURTHER, PREJUDICE WILL RESULT TO PLAINTIFF. THE UNAVAILABILITY OF A TIMELY REPORT FROM DR. RUFF PRIOR TO TRIAL WILL UNFAIRLY PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS' ABILITY TO ANALYZE AND REBUT ITS FINDINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Notice Of Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Subpoena For Ihss'S Records
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK T. PETERSEN, ESQ., 858.459.4400, FOR DEFENDANTS AHN AND TODD OPPENHEIMER; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS AHN AND TODD OPPENHEIMER's Motion To Compel Enforcement Of The Subpoena For IHSS' Records is Denied. Defendants have not show good cause as to why discovery should be permitted past the discovery cut off date. Defendants have not shown that Viktor Shoykhet received the required notification that his employment records were sought. The scope of the subpoena is overly broad. No monetary sanctions will be awarded. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Medical Examinations
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS TODD OPPENHEIMER AND ANH OPPENHEIMER'S Motion For Medical Examinations is Denied. Defendants have not demonstrated good cause to re-open discovery to take the mental examination of Plaintiff. (CCP 2024.050(b).) Defendants have not shown diligence in proceeding with discovery. Defendants were aware that Plaintiff's mental condition was at issue months prior to the filing of this action and that the medical records reviewed by Dr. Ruff were made available many months before he was first engaged as an expert by the defense. Defendants' delay in commencing review of the medical records made available by the Plaintiffs is inconsistent with the level of diligence which would warrant re-opening discovery. (See, e.g., Johnson v. Alameda County Medical Center (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 521 (when the moving party delays commencing discovery, the trial court did not abuse discretion by refusing to re-open discovery).) Further, prejudice will result to Plaintiff. The unavailability of a timely report from Dr. Ruff prior to trial will unfairly prejudice Plaintiffs' ability to analyze and rebut its findings. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
